explanation: Risk signals detected: Display Name / Local Part vs. Actual Email Mismatch, Domain Mismatch (Claimed Org vs. Actual Domain), Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Use of URL Shorteners, Urgency and Threats, Requests for Credentials or Payment.
subject: Your Netflix membership is about to be suspended
llm_explanation: This email impersonates Netflix but was sent from customer-billing-portal.com, which is not an official Netflix domain. The urgency language is a clear indicator of a social engineering attempt to harvest billing credentials. Do not click any links in this email.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Lookalike Sender Domain (Typosquatting), Lookalike Domain (Typosquatting), Urgency and Threats, Requests for Credentials or Payment.
subject: Urgent: Suspicious login attempt detected on your account
llm_explanation: This email claims to be a security alert from Chase but originates from chase-secure-banking-alert.com, a lookalike typosquatted domain. The combination of failed authentication and requests for urgent verification indicates credential phishing. Report this to IT security.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Requests for Credentials or Payment, Too-Good-To-Be-True Offers.
subject: Congratulations! You won the $15,000 Global Excellence Scholarship
llm_explanation: This email promises a $15,000 scholarship from studentfunds-verify.net, a domain with no relation to legitimate academic institutions. Offering unsolicited large monetary prizes in exchange for verifying details is a standard advance-fee scam tactic. Delete this email.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Domain Mismatch (Claimed Org vs. Actual Domain), Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Lookalike Sender Domain (Typosquatting), Lookalike Domain (Typosquatting), Urgency and Threats, Requests for Credentials or Payment.
subject: Critical Update: Renew your Office 365 student subscription
llm_explanation: This email impersonates Microsoft Office but utilizes the lookalike domain micros0ft-office-upgrade.com (using a zero '0' instead of 'o'). It asks to renew a subscription immediately, which is a credential harvesting attack. Do not log in.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Display Name / Local Part vs. Actual Email Mismatch, Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Use of URL Shorteners, Urgency and Threats.
subject: Action Required: Sign your university tax document now
llm_explanation: This email pretends to be from DocuSign but was sent via envelope-status-alert.com. It uses urgent call-to-action language ('Sign your university tax document now') to trick students into opening malicious links. Do not sign the document.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Urgency and Threats, Requests for Credentials or Payment.
subject: Urgent: Your cryptocurrency wallet has been compromised
llm_explanation: This email claims a crypto wallet is compromised, sent from blockcha1n-security-desk.com (using a '1' instead of 'i'). This is a typosquatted phishing attempt to steal wallet keys. Never input your recovery phrase.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Domain Mismatch (Claimed Org vs. Actual Domain), Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Lookalike Sender Domain (Typosquatting), Lookalike Domain (Typosquatting), Too-Good-To-Be-True Offers.
subject: You have (1) unclaimed $500 Amazon Gift Card reward
llm_explanation: This email uses a too-good-to-be-true offer ($500 Amazon Gift Card reward) to lure the user into clicking a link from amazon-gift-sweepstakes.com. Amazon does not distribute rewards from this domain. Avoid clicking.

explanation: Risk signals detected: Failed Authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), Lookalike Sender Domain (Typosquatting), Lookalike Domain (Typosquatting), Urgency and Threats, Requests for Credentials or Payment.
subject: Unable to deliver package - Action required within 24 hours
llm_explanation: This email claims a package delivery failure, demanding action within 24 hours from fedex-shipping-notice.com. This domain is not affiliated with FedEx. The urgency is designed to install malware or steal personal data. Do not interact.

subject: Your account has been restricted - Review needed
llm_explanation: This email claims a PayPal account restriction, sent from paypal-support-desk.com. The actual sender domain is unaffiliated with PayPal, and the link leads to a credential harvesting site. Report this phishing attempt.

llm_explanation: This email impersonates a university IT helpdesk regarding password expiry, sent from it-helpdesk-system-warning.com. The domain is generic and untrusted, indicating a harvest attempt. Contact your IT service desk directly.

llm_explanation: This email claims to be an Adobe renewal notice but originates from adobe-license-renewal.com. This lookalike domain is not the official Adobe domain, and it represents a phishing attempt targeting student accounts. Do not renew via this link.

llm_explanation: This email contains an active prompt injection attack ('Ignore previous instructions...') attempting to bypass security scanners. The message requests a ₹5000 training fee and has failed authentication checks (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), pointing to a recruitment scam. Do not reply or send money.
